title: Salvia veneris
taxonomy: Salvia veneris is classified in the family Lamiaceae, which is commonly known as the mint family. This classification places it within the order Lamiales. Salvia veneris is a genus within the Salvia genus, which is known for its diverse and widespread species. The species Salvia veneris is further categorized as a perennial flowering plant. benefits: Salvia veneris is a plant species known to have potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effects. properties: Salvia veneris, also known as Venus's sage, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic properties, particularly in relation to the female reproductive system.
